* Personal and professional development.


Are you a driven, dedicated, and enthusiastic individual who wants to work in a friendly and rewarding environment? If so, we are looking for someone like you! The following position is open to anyone residing in Winnipeg and surrounding areas with valid work authorization.
  
POSITION SUMMARY
  
The Data and Research Analyst collects, analyzes, and interprets data for reporting, monitors KPIs and business metrics for the Experience Division. The role demands strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to turn complex data into actionable insights through research, problem\-solving, and cross\-department collaboration.
  
  

KEY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
  
Data Gathering and Preparation
  
* Access, extracts, and completes data from various internal systems such as data cubes, spreadsheets, websites, and external sources.
* Cleans and prepares data by identifying missing values, duplicates, and inconsistencies to ensure data quality and integrity.
* Maintains and organizes data repositories for easy access and reproducibility.


Reporting and Data Visualization
  
* Creates meaningful visualizations, dashboards, and reports for internal and external stakeholders.
* Creates recurring and complex ad\-hoc reports that track utilization, key performance indicators and business metrics.
* Develops PowerPoint decks, PDFs, and report templates for internal and external Stakeholders.
* Develops and prepares productivity reports for the Experience Division departments.
* Creates and tests web reports for the Experience Division.


Research and Analysis
  
* Analyzes report requests to assess their feasibility and requirements.
* Determines reporting thresholds and privacy limitations to ensure adherence to confidentiality guidelines.
* Conducts research and reviews on behalf of experience division.
* Analyzes trends within the insurance industry and reports them to the Manager, Business Solutions.


Communication/Customer Service
  
* Responds to internal and external client inquiries relating to data and reporting.
* Conducts presentations, information sessions and Q\&A sessions on subject matter expert knowledge to internal and external stakeholders.
* Acknowledges and communicates Group \& Change information within the reporting team on a regular basis.


Team Collaboration and Support
  
* Helps train new team member as part of the onboarding process.
* Supports internal control functions, identifying process inefficiencies and working with applicable areas to develop solutions.
* Participates in various projects and collaborates with interdepartmental teams to provide subject matter expert perspectives and analytical support during all phases of organizational projects.
* Participates in the development of strategic and operational objectives at a departmental level.


Data Governance and Documentation
  
* Creates and maintains proper documentation of data sources and methodologies.
* Supports data quality initiatives by identifying and resolving discrepancies or error and provide suggestions for improvement.
* Ensures compliance with privacy and data security policies and guidelines (especially when working with sensitive or regulated data).


Professional Development and Learning
  
* Stays current with new tools, technologies, research methodologies, best practices, and industry trends.
* As approved by Manager, Business Solutions, initiate such personal and professional development, as may be required, to remain effective in this position.


QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS
  
* Dedicated to the principles of exceptional service; committed to responding to, anticipating and addressing customer needs, for both internal and external customers.
* Post\-secondary degree, diploma or certificate in Data Science, Statistics, Economics, Business Administration or related field.
* 1\-3 year experience working with structured and unstructured datasets
* Familiarity with project management and visualization tools (e.g. Jira, Power BI)
* Experience in research methodologies and statistical analysis
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. Knowledge of the principles and practices of quantitative analysis including cost benefit analysis, statistical analysis and data visualization best practices.
* Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage and prioritize several key activities on an ongoing basis. Ability to work in an unstructured environment. Requires minimal supervision and direction.
* Exceptional interpersonal skills with ability to deal with all levels within the organization in a team environment. Functions well in a team setting.
* Superior written and verbal communication skills including experience conducting presentations.
* Thorough knowledge of office productivity tools, including Microsoft 365\. Adobe Acrobat, SharePoint, and various database applications.
* Experience working in the Health Benefits and Insurance industry is an asset.
* Knowledge of programming languages such as SQL and Python an asset.


Ready to Apply? We are excited to meet you!
  
If you would like to join our team, you are invited to apply by submitting your resume and cover letter by clicking the "Apply" button below by April 1, 2026\.
  
**To learn more visit:** https://www2\.mb.bluecross.ca/about\-us/careers
  
We appreciate the interest expressed by all applicants and acknowledge the effort put into the application process, however only those selected for an interview will be contacted.
  
  

Manitoba Blue Cross is committed to the principles of diversity, equity \& inclusion and to promoting opportunities in hiring for everyone. We want our workforce to reflect the diverse community we serve, and we're committed to creating a fair and respectful workplace.
  
We encourage all qualified candidates to apply. If you require an accommodation during the hiring process, please let us know.

Clinical Service Coordinator - Sexual Assault Recovery and Healing Program
description:
The SARAH (Sexual Assault Recovery and Healing) Program provides support to those affected by sexual violence through the SARAH Crisis Program and SARAH Counselling and Support Program. The primary responsibilities of this position include clinical oversight of the SARAH Program, conducting one\-on\-one counselling and intakes, support groups, and workshops for survivors of sexual violence and those who support survivors. Additionally, providing support to the SARAH Crisis Program delivery, training, and to our volunteers.

**IN THIS ROLE, YOUR FOCUS WOULD BE:** 

* Clinical oversight of the SARAH counselling and crisis program and provision of support to the counselling team.
* Coordination of SARAH program developments and leadership when executing objectives, including but not limited to groups, events, webinars, workshops, trainings, etc.
* Training and on\-boarding of new employees under the SARAH umbrella.
* Work in collaboration with Executive Director to ensure program is operating in alignment with our agreed mandates.
* Delivery of SARAH Counselling programming including one\-on\-one counselling, intakes, support groups, and workshops for survivors and/or service providers.
* Delivery and oversight of Project SMOKE (Sexual Misconduct Outreach Knowledge \& Empowerment), providing support, workshops, and resources to members of CAF.
* Support of SARAH Crisis Program conducting debriefing, follow\-up, volunteer training, in\-services, and on\-call coordination rotation.
* Advocating for survivors as they encounter various systems, including justice, medical, CFS, EIA, housing, and involvement with the Canadian Armed Forces.
* Complete appropriate participant case notes and accompanying materials.
* Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of appropriate community services, in order to provide information and referrals.
* Work in collaboration with existing team members to further program development and expansion of Survivor’s Hope services within the region.
* Contribute to monthly newsletter highlighting upcoming events, and recent/relevant programming.
* Represent Survivor’s Hope at a wide variety collateral agencies, networks, committees, stakeholders, and communities within region to build partnerships.
* Oversee program resource allocation including purchasing program supplies, maintaining expense records, and proposing new project budgets.
* Keep clear documentation of activities and assist in compiling program reports for funders

**WHAT YOU BRING:** 

· Relevant post\-secondary education in human services or social services (ie: BA psychology, sociology, BSW, B.Ed, B. Sc, MMFT , Psych Nurse , diploma in counselling, etc. )

· Additional training certificates in ASIST, safeTALK, Mental Health First Aid, Trauma\-Informed Care considered an asset

· Registration with applicable professional designation an asset

OR

· Two\-four years equivalent experience working in social services settings, additional education, knowledge, and/or training may be considered

*·* Knowledge and understanding of the Child and Family Services Act, *Personal Health Information Act and Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act.*

· Experience facilitating support groups and workshops.

· Experience with the Canadian Armed Forces community an asset.

· Knowledge of mental health problems and illnesses, including signs, symptoms, and treatment models

· Knowledge of co\-occurring disorders (addictions, mental health \& trauma)

· Experience coordinating services and working from an empowerment model.

· Experience creating events and programs

· Strong organizational skills with an attention to detail and accuracy

· Strong written and verbal communication skills.

· Strong intersectional feminist, anti\-oppressive and anti\-racist analysis.

· Demonstrated understanding of the social and systemic root causes of sexual violence and the context of increased risk of violence and differential impacts of trauma for specific groups

· Demonstrated skills in Microsoft Office Suite (word, excel, power point).

· Proficiency in online platforms including Zoom, MS Teams, and GSuite.

· Ability to work independently, with indirect support, as well as contribute positively to a team.

· Knowledge of local community resources would be an asset.

· Valid driver’s licence and access to reliable vehicle required.

· Child, adult abuse check, criminal record, and vulnerable sector checks prior to start date as well as reliable reference statements are required.

**WHAT WE OFFER:**

· Support from a diverse and qualified team of coworkers, overseen by the Executive Director and Board of Directors

· A lasting reputation of quality programming and strong relationships with our many community partners

· A flexible and rewarding, hybrid work experience that values a healthy work/life balance

· A fiscally sound and established organization that allows for innovative program development and endless opportunities for growth

description:
**Requisition ID:** 399374


**Position Number:** 20057806


**Posting End Date:** March 27, 2026


**City:** Winnipeg


**Employer:** Seven Oaks General Hospital


**Site:** Seven Oaks General Hospital


**Department / Unit:** Wellness Institute Clinic

**Union:** Non Union

**FTE:** 1\.00


**Anticipated Shift:** Days;Evenings;Weekends


**Work Arrangement:** In Person


**Daily Hours Worked:** 7\.75


**Annual Base Hours:** 2015  

Salary is commensurate with education and qualifications.

  




Grow your career in the Winnipeg Health Region! Our team provides a spectrum of health care services through an integrated network of sites, services and organizations. We’re united by a shared commitment to excellent and equitable health care.

**Position Overview**
---------------------



The Clinical and Fitness Support Specialist reports to the Manager of Clinical Operations and plays a central role in coordinating key chronic disease programs, as well as associated assessment and testing services (DEXA, RMR, VO₂max). They work closely with the manager to develop new and innovative clinical services and programs, and to review and update existing programs to ensure they remain effective, evidence\-informed, and aligned with organizational priorities.  

In addition, the role collaborates with the Health \& Fitness team to support program development, staff training, research integration, and quality assurance processes. This position promotes high standards of evidence\-informed practice, participant\-centered care, and innovative program delivery across both clinical and health \& fitness initiatives.

**Experience**
--------------


* 4 years’ experience in a health\-related setting.
* Experience in the development, implementation and evaluation of policies, standards, protocols and/or frameworks.
* Experience in coordinating entry level positions.
* Experience in reviewing core messaging/content created by health or fitness personnel.
* Experience in and knowledge of principles and techniques of research and analysis, with the ability to apply to program and policy evaluation preferred.
* Developed and coordinated clinical and fitness programs, including chronic disease management initiatives.
**Education (Degree/Diploma/Certificate)**
------------------------------------------


* Post\-secondary degree in health related, kinesiology or health sciences field required.
**Certification/Licensure/Registration**
----------------------------------------


* Current CSEP\-CEP or ACSM\-CEP certification in good standing.
**Qualifications and Skills**
-----------------------------


* Supported program budget tracking and resource allocation to ensure efficient use of funds.
* Contributed to program enhancement initiatives focused on quality assurance and continuous improvement.
* Assisted with research coordination and interdisciplinary projects.
* Provided staff training and mentorship to promote evidence\-based practice.
* Managed multiple priorities and projects with professionalism and efficiency.
* Strong knowledge of evidence\-based clinical exercise practices and chronic disease management.
* Expertise in clinical assessment and testing protocols, including DEXA, RMR, and VO₂max.
* Proficiency in program design, implementation, and evaluation using evidence\-based approaches.
* Excellent communication, organizational, analytical, and problem\-solving skills.
* Ability to collaborate effectively across departments and multidisciplinary teams.
* Knowledge of clinical care and fitness trends, issues and strategies preferred.
* Knowledge of chronic disease trends, issues and strategies preferred.
**Physical Requirements**
-------------------------


* Must be willing to work some flexible hours.

  





**This position requires a current satisfactory Criminal Records Check (including Vulnerable Sector Search), Child Abuse Registry Check and Adult Abuse Registry Check as conditions of employment. The successful candidate will be responsible for any service charges incurred. A security check is considered current if it was obtained no more than six (6\) months prior to the start of employment.**

  



Please note that an employee is not permitted to hold two or more positions across the WRHA legal entity that combine to equal more than 1\.0 EFT. The WRHA legal entity includes Churchill Health Centre, Deer Lodge Centre, Golden West Centennial Lodge, Grace Hospital, Middlechurch Home of Winnipeg, Pan Am Clinic, River Park Gardens, Victoria General Hospital, WRHA corporate programs, and WRHA community health services.

  



Interviewed candidates may be called upon to participate in a skills assessment.

  



Any application received after the closing time will not be included in the competition.

description:
Ready to join an outstanding culture that cares about diversity and inclusion, corporate social responsibility, and gives the freedom to innovate, influence decisions, and chart an exciting career?  

  

CGI is more than just an IT company. Be part of a team that supports the local community with the ability to draw on global best\-in\-class talent! Benefits include a share purchase program, profit sharing, wellness credits, training and development programs, and flexible work schedules and locations.  

  

CGI\-Winnipeg is seeking a Project Control Officer who has a minimum of 1\-2 years of working experience in project coordination and/or project administration, preferably in a Consulting or Information Technology environment supporting Project Managers. This is an intermediate project control / entry project management role that will provide support to several Project Managers and teams providing information technology services to our clients.

**Your future duties and responsibilities**


. Ensure project time entries are valid for projects, and task descriptions reference project WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) tasks from MS Project plans, and complete weekly updates of MS Project plans  

. Complete monthly Time summary roll\-ups, and initiate client project reporting  

. Manage and organize all project contractual information, including Accounting Summaries and Change Requests, and ensure all documents are properly completed and filed with Finance and on Ensemble/SharePoint  

. Maintain the Project Deliverables Checklist for all projects, and ensure that all documents are filed on Ensemble along with appropriate client approvals  

. Ensure all CPMF\-mandated documents and checklists are filed on Ensemble, and that a Project Closure Checklist is completed and filed for each project  

. Support the creation and maintenance and proper filing of project status reports and external and internal meeting minutes  

. Support the project teams with other document or material development as required for the various projects  

. Provide logistical support to project teams for submitting resource assignment requests, booking meetings, maintaining distribution lists, and following up with assigned resources on action items  

. Acting as the main point of contact with the Project Administration Team to ensure consistent and efficient processing of all project administration tasks, such as new engagement setup, resource assignments, Time Reports, and adjustment requests  

. Integrate within the team, growing your project control skills into a junior project management role, while delivering operational excellence tied to our contractual obligations  

. Utilize latest AI tools to increase productivity, improve existing processes and identify potential time savings

**Required qualifications to be successful in this role**


. Post\-secondary education in Business Administration or a related field  

. 1\-2 years related experience in consulting/IT department supporting project managers  

. CAPM certification desired  

. Solid understanding of MS Office 365, including Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Visio, and Project  

. Experience with invoicing processes in a consulting/project\-related environment  

  

Professional Attributes:  

  

. Results\-oriented and highly motivated  

. Dedicated to professional growth  

. Passionate about excellent client delivery  

. Able to prioritize multiple requests, working to client timelines  

. Able to work in a team environment working toward common goals  

. Able to plan, develop and improve processes  

. Solid attention to detail  

. Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal  

  

Nice to Have:  

  

. General knowledge of accounting, project management, PeopleSoft, and process management  

. A clear understanding of the roles and responsibilities of the Project Administration Team within the CGI Winnipeg office.  

  

\#LI\-  

  

CGI is providing a reasonable estimate of the pay range for this role. The determination of this range includes various factors including but not limited to skill set level, geographic market, experience and training, and licenses and certifications. Compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $40,000 to $80,000\.

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**  

  

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…  

  

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.  

  

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.  

  

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well\-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.  

  

At CGI, we value the strength that diversity brings and are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone belongs. We collaborate with our clients to build more inclusive communities and empower all CGI partners to thrive. As an equal\-opportunity employer, being able to perform your best during the recruitment process is important to us. If you require an accommodation, please inform your recruiter.

title: Project Associate
description:
As a Project Associate, you will assist the Project Management Office (PMO) with the implementation of project/process initiatives by providing confidential administrative support.


### **Specific Responsibilities:**



Assist Project Managers and/or Business Analysts to lead cross\-functional, complex projects and/or analysis.


* Provide administrative support to Project Managers and/or Business Analysts including, but not limited to delivering and/or supporting delivery of core documentation for assigned projects, communication memos, preparation of presentations, and maintenance/monitoring of records and project workbooks.
* Collaborate with diverse teams in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment, balancing and aligning the needs of multiple project stakeholders.
* Organize, schedule and prioritize meetings related to project initiatives; creating agendas and recording minutes as required.
* Help identify project risks and assist in developing mitigation strategies.
* Liaise with staff, managers, and/or suppliers/vendors for information sharing and/or information exchange
* Assist in conducting business analysis, project and/or change management as required for the PMO.
* Process and maintain core documents, files, and reports for the PMO accurately and efficiently.
* Identify and recommend ideas/solutions to PMO Leadership, Project Managers and/or Business Analysts to assist with the efficient and effective delivery of project/process initiatives.
* Contribute to the development and/or enhancement of business analysis, project management, and change management best practices used by the PMO.
* Maintain general understanding of and adhere to project / change management and/or process improvement practices and methodologies.
* Actively participate in staff meetings, taking minutes and providing input when necessary.
* Ensure familiarity with and commitment to SCU's and PMO's vision, mission, and values.
* Maintain open communication and cooperation with staff to ensure a friendly and efficient work environment.

### **Qualifications:**


* Grade 12 Diploma plus 1 to 2 years of formal project or administrative related education.
* 4 to 6 years of administration related experience, or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Certified Associate in Project Management (CAPM) certification is an asset.
* **Essential Skills:**
* Business acumen \- maintain up to date knowledge of SCU's products and services
* Financial acumen – understanding of budgeting and financial reporting.
* Intermediate to advanced administrative proficiency:


	+ Organization and Time Management – ability to prioritize tasks, manage multiple deadlines, and stay organized.
	+ Communication – excellent written and verbal communication. skills to effectively interact with team members and stakeholders.
	+ Problem solving – ability to identify and assist in resolving project issues and challenges.
	+ Technical skills – proficiency in Microsoft and project management software and tools.
	+ Teamwork and Collaboration – ability to work effectively with diverse teams and individuals.
	+ Attention to detail – excellence in maintaining records, tracking progress, and ensuring accuracy.

We believe our employees should represent the diversity of our members and the communities in which we live and serve. If you require an accommodation for the recruitment or interview process (including alternate formats of materials, or accessible meeting rooms or other accommodation), please let us know and we’ll work with you to meet your needs.

We are committed to employing a diverse workforce and encourage all qualified individuals to apply. We thank all candidates for their interest, however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

description:
Darling International Canada Inc., is Canada's largest provider of rendering and recycling services to the food industry, operating six processing facilities across Canada. Darling Canada provides essential services for the efficient and environmentally responsible management of collecting, processing and recycling edible and inedible animal by\-products. Darling is committed to environmental sustainability, superior quality and excellent customer service.

**POSITION SUMMARY:**


Reporting to the Product Safety \& Quality Assurance Coordinator, the Lab Technician will play a key role in supporting and executing the product safety and quality vision of Darling International Canada Inc. The role will ensure the effective execution of laboratory analysis and communication of results, while supporting the laboratory quality and PSQA Programs. This is a 12 month contract role with possibility of extension.

**POSITION TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**

**Analytical Lab**

* Active participation in laboratory \& site health \& safety program, able to provide potential solutions and corrective actions for issues identified
* Ensure product and analysis processes meet product quality and safety standards prior to shipment
* Communicate with site leadership and operators to provide direction when samples are out of spec, or trending towards being out of spec
* Generate customer\-facing documentation that is timely, accurate and professional
* Generate laboratory results that are accurate and precise and ensure the timely data entry of all samples and records
* Ensures internal and third party product testing is performed, according to defined schedules and sampling programs, to ensure compliance with regulatory and customer requirements (i.e. APPI Program, ruminant testing, moisture testing, etc.)
* Ensure lab inspections, calibrations and preventative maintenance activities are completed according to schedule, take corrective action where available
* Complete product safety program monitoring, verification, deviation, record review \& inspection documentation as required
* Other duties as assigned

**HACCP Plans**

* Supports the implementation, verification, reassessment, maintenance and storage of site level HACCP plans, prerequisite programs, and supporting documentation
* Supports the monitoring and verification of product safety programs and facility practices, and appropriate actions to ensure the integrity of the HACCP system
* Identifies site trends related to product safety (foreign material, complaints, non\-conformances, etc.) as well as opportunities for continuous improvement initiatives
* Provides support to the Product Safety \& Quality Assurance Coordinator when dealing with CFIA inspectors, third party auditors, and customers
* Assists with internal audits conducted by the Corporate PSQA team annually
* Ensures all matters pertaining to product safety and quality are promptly reported to the Product Safety \& Quality Assurance Coordinator
* Other duties as assigned

**REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS:**

* University or college studies in chemistry, microbiology, science, agriculture management and/or related field
* 1\-3\+ years' experience in a Quality Assurance, Product Safety and/or Regulatory field preferred;
* Experience working in the food or agriculture processing industry an asset

**REQUIREMENTS:**

* Strong health \& safety mindset, demonstrating the ability to maintain the highest standards of health \& safety during all tasks
* Willing to work in noisy environments of varying temperature.
* Ability to lift and/or move up to 50 pounds, occasional outside work during all seasons, and at heights
* Ability to work independently, and as part of Lab, with minimal supervision
* Comfortable to work cross\-functionally, communicating directly with a variety of department members
* Strong ability to communicate in English, including verbal, written, and email communication
* Strong mathematics and data management skills
* Proficient skills in MS Office applications (Word, Excel and Outlook)
* Strong organizational, analytical and problem solving skills
* Ability to analyze and interpret information and communicate these results to others
* Ability to work with accuracy and attention to detail while multitasking, and the ability to work to tight and changing deadlines
* Able to work in a fast paced, changing environment with minimal supervision
* Possession of a positive, professional work ethic

**WHAT WE OFFER:**

* Competitive Salary
* Employee Assistance Program
* Flexible Health and Dental Benefit Plan

description:
**\*This is a term position until April 30, 2027\***



Financial Services provides all accounting services and financial reporting for the University of Winnipeg. The **Research and Grants Accounting Assistant is a full\-time term** role that supports the accounting and administration of research and grant accounts.


**Responsibilities:**


* Monitors research account transactions for over\-expenditures and unusual transactions.
* Works with Researchers and the Research Office to correct errors and resolve over\-expended accounts.
* Assists in reviewing grant accounts to ensure that they are current and funds are returned to grantor for dormant accounts.
* Provides day\-to\-day support and financial guidance to researchers with respect to their research accounts.
* Reviews and approves all research expenditures for eligibility, based on expense eligibility guidelines from Funding Agencies, and enters expenditures into Business Central for payment.
* Reviews all payments and purchases from research and designated fund accounts for availability of funds prior to payment, authorizes payment vouchers for availability of funds.
* Assists with preparing accurate and timely annual and periodic external financial statements for funding agencies.
* Assists with inputting award data into templates.
* Assists with reconciling reports to award accounts.
* Assists with obtaining authorizing signatures for reports.
* Sets up accounts for all approved, legitimate research projects and designated fund accounts.
* Establishes and maintains files for each grant and designated fund account.
* Records grant payments received.
* Reviews travel expense claims to ensure adherence to university policies and procedures.
* Preparation of billings to granting agencies as required.
* Monitors grant receivable balances and follows up when required.


**Qualifications:**


* High school completion.
* Current enrollment in the CPA program or equivalent.
* Must have at least 2 years related accounting experience.
* Experience with Research or other public sector Grants would be an asset.
* Experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365/Business Central considered an asset.
* Must have Experience and skills in computer technology.
* Proficient in Microsoft Office with working knowledge of MS Excel.
* Must have effective oral and written communication skills.
* Must have superior problem solving and analytical skills
* Must have the ability to work well and build relationships with people from a variety of backgrounds.
* Good organizational ability is required to meet deadlines.
* The ability to balance the needs of researchers to complete their work with the accountability needs of the funding agencies and the university.


**An equivalent combination of education, experience, skills, knowledge and abilities may be considered.**

* Must be legally entitled to work in Canada.

description:
Clearpoint Health Network (CHN) is one of Canada’s largest trusted provider of independent surgical and multi\-disciplinary medical centres across Canada. Working in close collaboration with hospitals, insurance providers, regional health organizations and workers’ compensation boards, Clearpoint delivers high\-quality care across Canada meeting the needs of patients, while alleviating capacity pressure on public health systems.  


Maples Surgical Centre a division of Clearpoint Health Network Inc. We offer various services, including surgeries and consultations, to relieve some of the pressure on the public healthcare system. Maples Surgical Centre, Specialties include Orthopaedics, Gynecology, Plastics, ENT, Urology, Spine, Cosmetics and others as required.  


Maples Surgical Centre, a division of Clearpoint Health Network is now recruiting for 1 Full Time Medical Device Reprocessing Technician to Join Our Team  

**Benefits:*** Free Parking
* Competetive Wage
* Health and Dental Benefits
* Up to 3% match RRSP
* Paid Vacation \& Personal Time Off
* Varied shifts, Monday to Friday and Weekends

  

**Position Summary:**
The medical device reprocessing (MDR) technician is responsible for the cleaning, disassembly, reassembly and sterilization of surgical supplies, equipment and reusable medical devices. Principles of aseptic technique shall be used, and routine infection control practices shall be followed. The MDR technician will ensure that reusable medical devices are in proper working order, perform biological indicator, chemical indicator and other quality assurance testing and report problems to the supervisor.  


MDR technicians are on their feet, walking or standing for much of their shift. They may be required to bend, reach, lift and move equipment and supplies. MDR technicians are exposed to strong chemicals and bio\-hazardous materials while doing their jobs. Their work areas can be noisy and busy and, at times, can be challenging or stressful.  

**Key Responsibilities:*** Collect, disassemble, clean, disinfect, assemble, function test, package, sterilize, store and distribute reusable medical devices, equipment and supplies used in the surgical suite.
* Operate and maintain a variety of equipment used to reprocess reusable medical devices.
* Assemble and provide reusable medical device sets and carts as ordered for a variety of surgical procedures.
* Inventory, monitor, order, replenish and rotate supplies in the work area.
* Cleaning and disinfecting work areas, and inspecting and maintaining supplies to ensure supplies are functioning and surgical areas are well stocked.
* Follow established safety and infection control protocols.
* Document and report on activities within the work area.
* Provide leadership and guidance to co\-workers.
* Maintain surgical inventory, and stock supplies as per protocol.
* Support peri\-operative suite as requested.
* Assist with OR cleaning and turnovers between cases.

 **Qualifications:*** Completion of an accredited Medical Device Reprocessing program.
* Must be able to read, write, speak and understand English fluently.
* Recent experience in an acute hospital medical device reprocessing environment as asset.



Clearpoint Health Network Inc is committed to nurturing a diverse workforce representative of the communities within which we operate. We encourage and are pleased to consider all qualified candidates, without regard to race, colour, citizenship, religion, sex, marital / family status, sexual orientation, gender identity, aboriginal status, age, disability or persons who may require an accommodation, to apply.  


For accommodation requests during the hiring process, please contact HR@clearpointhealth.ca for further information.  

*Clearpoint Health Network will only contact those candidates being considered for an interview. We sincerely appreciate your interest in this opportunity and thank you for your application. By submitting your resume, you agree to Clearpoint Health Network sharing your information with representatives of the organization responsible for the hiring process. Clearpoint Health Network will keep your resume on file and protect your personal information in accordance with governing privacy legislation.*

best as we continue to build a company that champions safety, supports innovation, and delivers on our commitment to customer  

service \- while actively fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace reflective of the communities we serve.

community.

nature of work, operational requirements and work location.


***Position Overview:***

description:
**Company Description**  

We are SGS – the world's leading testing, inspection and certification company. We are recognized as the global benchmark for sustainability, quality and integrity. Our 99,600 employees operate a network of 2,600 offices and laboratories, working together to enable a better, safer and more interconnected world.

 **Job Description** **This position operates on a 24/7 basis. Employees are required to work on rotating shifts ranging from 8 \- 12 hours in length with differing schedules including day shifts, afternoons shifts, night shifts and weekends. Those role also requires travelling outside the area for 2\-3 days at a time.**

**This position requires you to have a valid driver’s license.**


Daily Activities:

* Log\-in, organizing, storing, distribution, and disposing of incoming samples and after final inspections
* Clean grain samples using dockage carter/sieves and prepare for grading
* Picking up and delivering samples from various locations to office or vice versa
* Operate, maintain, and calibrate laboratory equipment (moisture meters, scales, dockage, testers, protein testers, ELISA readers). Monitor all equipment according to desired standards before performing the analysis
* Perform analyses such as moisture, protein, dockage, test weight, mycotoxins, germination tests, etc.
* Analysis of miscellaneous other branch specific laboratory analyses (malt barley variety identification, audits, etc.)
* Maintaining/monitoring of basic laboratory supplies (gloves, dust masks, filter papers etc.).
* Cleaning of work areas (dusting, sweeping, etc.).
* Follow all requirements according to the Canada Grain Act and Canada Grain regulations.
* Follow CFIA protocol for sampling, CGC standards for sampling, SGS SOPs, any other client specific specs.
* Issuing initial and final reports related to food, container, industrial, etc. inspections (i.e. Government Inspection Services, GIS).
* Assist in preparing on\-site final analysis reports (i.e. Perform the inspection and complete the inspection report)

 **Qualifications**  

* A minimum of a High School diploma
* 1 – 3 years of experience in a laboratory or Agricultural setting is an asset

  

**Additional Information** **Salary:** $23\-$25 Per Hour

**Some benefit highlights:**

* 80% coverage Health, Dental and Vision
* Paid time off
* RRSP Program Eligibility

description:
Permanent Full Time

* 

  

We are looking for a Senior ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) Investment Analyst. This role is located in Winnipeg. **Role description**
The Senior ESG Investment Analyst will aid in enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of reporting that supports the delivery of Canada Life’s parent company, Great\-West Lifeco’s, ESG commitments. This includes Lifeco’s ambitions to achieve net zero for operations and financed emissions for certain asset classes in the general account by 2050\.  

The Senior ESG Investment Analyst will be responsible for coordinating the collection and analysis of sustainability investment data related to investee companies in the general account and will support Lifeco’s regional and affiliate investment teams. **What you will do**  

Support the ESG Investment Reporting Manager in data collection, verification, and analysis to produce various ESG disclosures and climate\-related reporting:* Design, implement, and maintain processes that will support the timely delivery of complete and accurate ESG investment data, such as financed emissions, investment analytics, and other climate\-related KPIs, to stakeholders to enable critical investment analysis capabilities.
* Assist in managing, tracking and updating key performance indicators relevant to internal stakeholders and public goals (such as the Great\-West Lifeco’s interim climate and net zero ambitions), and assist in the production of related presentation materials and internal progress updates.
* Assist the implementation of a project to automate financed emissions calculations, ensuring successful delivery of the results to meet regulatory requirements.
* Contribute to the development of the Company’s climate transition plan through data\-driven insights and reporting as well as analyzing financial information and investment results for senior management and business partners.
* Collaborate with internal teams across the organization to develop value\-added ESG analysis on the Company’s consolidated investment portfolio, supporting the Company’s climate transition plan and various board and committee reporting.
* Support regulatory reporting requirements, including CDP and OSFI submissions.
* Work with strategic external partners to evaluate and implement third party tools and datasets.
* Support the development and implementation of ESG reporting frameworks and methodologies, ensuring compliance with applicable regulations, standards and guidelines such as PCAF, IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards, OSFI, EU SFDR, etc.
* Participate in and complete ad hoc projects and reports, as required.

 **What you will bring** **Knowledge \& Expertise*** Bachelor's or Master's degree in one of the following: commerce, finance, accounting, actuarial, business analytics, computer science, statistics and mathematics or a related field
* 3\-5 years' experience in finance, consulting, investments, corporate reporting, sustainability, or a related field
* Knowledge or experience in one or more of the following fields is an asset: business, management, risk management, sustainable development, sustainable finance, consulting, finance, capital markets, corporate reporting, investor relations, governance, climate change.

 **Attributes*** Highly knowledgeable in data management concepts and best practices, and experienced in automating processes
* Strong data analytics and visualization skills, comfortable working with large datasets, and handling multiple sources of data
* Expert user of Excel
* Keen analytical thinking, bring detail oriented, and having a continuous learning mindset (professional curiosity)
* Ability to build strong relationships, internally and externally, with a wide range of stakeholders
* Proficiency with SQL query tools and Microsoft Power BI is an asset
* Familiarity on ESG related matters and/or with ESG\-related standards and frameworks including EU SFDR, GRI, PCAF and TCFD is an asset
* 


The base salary for this position is between **$54,300 \- $81,400** annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.


Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

**Grow with Canada Life**

*We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well\-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.*

*We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!*

**What we offer**:


We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full\-time or part\-time permanent team member:

* **Career Development**: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry\-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
* **Health \& Wellness:** Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well\-being.
* **Time Off:** In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day.
* **Financial Security:** Company\-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
* **Rewards and Recognition:** Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
* **Emphasis on Community**: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.

Learn more about Canada Life.


*We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact* *talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com**. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.*

description:
At CLEAResult, we lead the transition to a sustainable, equitable, and carbon\-neutral energy\-efficient future for our communities and our planet. We do that by creating a people\-first culture built on trust, accountability, and transparency; where every employee – regardless of position, role, or identity is treated with respect and given an equal chance to thrive.  

  

Additionally, you will enjoy:
  

* Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance with company premium assistance
* Life, AD\&D, and long\-term disability coverage paid for by the company
* RRSP company match
* Paid vacation, sick and personal time
* Paid company holidays
* Paid Volunteer Time; giving back to our communities is important to us
* Employee Referral Bonus Program
* Employee Recognition Program – convert your recognition points into gift cards
* Employee Assistance Program – offers benefits to help you manage daily responsibilities
* Access to training opportunities to advance further in your career
**Job Description**

**We are looking for a talented individual…**


We are looking for a talented individual to join our team as a Launch Project Strategist to directly impact a mission\-driven, energetic workforce who are changing the way people use energy! In this role, you will be responsible for ensuring that new projects are effectively and efficiently launched to align with our Statement of Work (SOW) and exceed our client expectations. The Launch Project Strategist will be the point person for all teams during the launch, until full operation is assumed by the delivery team.

**You’re a great fit if you can…**

* Own end‑to‑end program launches from contract award through go‑live, with limited post‑launch support as needed.
* Develop and manage comprehensive launch plans using standardized tools, ensuring alignment to scope, timelines, and SOW requirements.
* Lead multiple concurrent launches, balancing priorities across varying timelines and complexities.
* Coordinate internal readiness activities, including resourcing, staffing plans, documentation, and cross‑functional dependencies.
* Serve as the primary point of contact during launch, partnering closely with clients and internal stakeholders to manage expectations and drive alignment.
* Monitor launch progress against milestones, proactively identifying risks, constraints, or dependencies and escalating issues as needed.
* Facilitate internal and external kickoff meetings, status check‑ins, and milestone reviews to maintain momentum and accountability.
* Navigate competing priorities and differing viewpoints, resolving conflicts and driving decisions to keep launches on track.
* Ensure launches are delivered on time and on budget, adapting plans as needed to address evolving client or program requirements.
* Conduct post‑launch reviews, share lessons learned, and contribute to continuous improvement of launch processes and best practices.

**Exciting candidates will have…**

* Post‑secondary education in Business, Communications, Project Management, or a related field.
* 3\+ years experience managing projects, implementations, or program launches in complex, cross‑functional environments.
* Operational experience in energy efficiency, utilities, environmental services, or public‑sector programs preferred.
* Proven ability to lead multi‑stakeholder initiatives with shifting timelines, priorities, and requirements.
* Strong client‑facing experience, including leading meetings, managing expectations, and navigating ambiguity.
* Demonstrated track record of execution, delivering results, not just plans.
* Hands‑on experience with project tracking tools (e.g., Excel, Asana, or similar).
* Comfortable owning timelines and milestones with limited formal budget responsibility.
* Exceptional communication, problem‑solving, and conflict‑navigation skills, with the confidence to push back constructively.
* Adaptable, resilient, and able to pivot quickly as client or program needs evolve.
* Collaborative, positive mindset with a strong interest in process improvement and program success.
* PMP or formal project management certification is a plus, not required.
* Willingness to flex workload during peak launch periods.

**Target Compensation**


$40\-$46/h

**Compensation Range**


$40\.50 \- $70\.10**Currency**


CAD**Type**


Hourly
Any offered salary is determined based on internal equity, internal salary ranges, market data/ranges, applicant’s skills and prior relevant experience, certain degrees and certifications (e.g. JD/technology), for example.
